title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Contract Lens: Transport Domain Response Owner
summary: Middleware runs in an unsafe order, async rejections are wrapped twice, error handlers use the wrong signature, dependencies are created per request, and multiple layers write responses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts transport domain response owner increment in composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.
Construct long-lived dependencies at startup and inject narrow capabilities without request-time pools, mutable service locators, or hidden globals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Transport Domain Response Owner · Theory Transport Domain Response…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7193 in a public incident API behind one regional edge proxy. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Transport Domain Response Owner contract for the supplied data.
